\documentclass{article}

\title{Paper With Abstract-Conclusion Mismatch}
\author{Overclaimer}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We present a groundbreaking method that achieves state-of-the-art results
on all major benchmarks, completely solving the open problem of X.
Our approach reduces error rates by 90\% compared to all existing methods
and generalizes perfectly to unseen domains.
\end{abstract}

\section{Introduction}
\label{sec:intro}

The problem of X remains challenging. We attempt a new approach.

\section{Method}
\label{sec:method}

We use a standard neural network with minor modifications.

\section{Results}
\label{sec:results}

Our method shows modest improvements on one benchmark dataset.

\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}

We presented an initial exploration of approach Y for problem X.
Results on one dataset show a small improvement over the baseline.
Further work is needed to validate on additional benchmarks and domains.
